content.full_text:
April 5, 1991

Sharon Barclay Kime
Turner & Sullivan
1755 Creekside Oaks Drive, Suite 290
Sacramento, California 95833

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-91-142

Dear Ms. Kime:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to you concerning the lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ Because you have not identified the person on whose behalf you are requesting advice, we consider your request to be one for informal assistance.2/

Your letter, dated February 28, 1991, correctly summarizes my advice that a physician organization would qualify as a lobbyist employer under the circumstances described in your letter.

For your reference, the definition of a lobbying coalition is contained in Regulation 18616.4 and, in part, states:

A lobbying coalition is a group of ten or more persons formed primarily to influence legislative or administrative action, whose members make payments to the coalition for the purpose of sharing the expenses of employing a lobbyist or contracting for the services of a lobbying firm. A bona fide

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)

federation, confederation or trade, labor or membership organization is not a lobbying coalition if it is ongoing in nature and its membership services are not limited to influencing legislative or administrative action....

Emphasis Added.

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience. If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
